title: Brother Act
theme: Jon Baker, three-time Jacket bridesmaid, finally cashes in — and the player who beats him to the line is his own brother, the defending champion. A four-day duel of Bakers settled by a single afternoon at PGA Catalunya.
narrative_structure: in_medias_res — open at R4 H6 (the decisive takeover), then loop back to how a Baker civil war reached that point
opening_hook: R4 H6 on the Stadium course: Jon Baker draws level with brother Alex on the Trophy and never trails again. The takeover that ended a 56-hole sibling tussle — and a four-TEG Jacket itch.
foreshadow: Jon Baker arrived as the eternal Jacket runner-up: silver in three of the last four TEGs without a win
Alex Baker arrived as defending Trophy champion, looking to retain
David Mullin arrived on the back of EIGHT Jackets and back-to-back wins of golf's most familiar green coat
John Patterson arrived as reigning Wooden Spoon holder, the one prize nobody wanted to defend

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.winner_or_loser: Jon Baker (143 pts, by 6 from Alex Baker)
competitions.how: Jon led after R1 and R2; Alex's R3 par-fest (b32) flipped him into outright lead at H13 (b14); Jon's steady R4 drew him level at H6 (b22) while Alex haemorrhaged shots in three separate clusters (b40, b35, b36) — final margin 6.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.winner_or_loser: Jon Baker (+70, by 3 from David Mullin)
competitions.how: Jon led wire-to-wire after grabbing it on R1 H3; Mullin briefly drew level at R3 H3 (b38) before Jon retook outright at R3 H6 (b09) and a Catalonia-PB R4 sealed it — dethroning the eight-time holder.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Patterson wore the dunce's cap from R1 H14 (b33) through R2; Mullin's R3 unravelling — a quadruple at H6 (b17) and the slip to last at H12 (b21) — handed it over, and he couldn't escape across R4.

headline_candidates: Jon Baker Tears Up the Stadium
Ninety, and a Record to Match
rounds.chosen_headline: Ninety, and a Record to Match
rounds.angle: Jon Baker opens with a 90 at the Stadium — nine shots better than his last visit and a new course record across six TEG visits. Patterson and Williams set the early Spoon tone with horror stretches.

headline_candidates: Patterson's 11, and a Course-Worst to Boot
rounds.angle: Jon goes lower still on the Tour course — 84 gross, another course record. Patterson posts an 11 on H15 and a course-worst 103 on the same card; Williams matches the 15th with a 10 of his own before stopping the bleeding with a birdie at 17.

On a new course at El Prat - Azul, Alex Baker's three-hole par streak (12-14) flips the Trophy on its head — first level at H1, then outright by H13. Meanwhile Mullin's quadruple at the 6th and slip at the 12th drop him into the Spoon, and Jon clings to the Jacket through a wobble.

headline_candidates: The Sixth Hole, And It Was Done
rounds.angle: Back on the Stadium for the Sunday finale. Jon draws level with Alex at H6 (b22) and never trails again. Alex bleeds three separate clusters of shots and posts his worst round to date; Mullin's late stumble locks in the Spoon. Jon's first Jacket, by three.

players.arc: The three-time Jacket bridesmaid finally wears the coat — and the Trophy on top. Two course records inside 48 hours, the wire-to-wire Jacket, and a R4 takeover at H6 against his own brother. No more silver.

The defending Trophy champion played the part for three rounds, seized the outright lead at El Prat, then dropped chunks of shots in R4 (clusters at 4-6, 12-14 and 16-18) — runner-up to his brother by six.

Arrived with eight Jackets and back-to-back wins; left without the coat AND with the Spoon. Dethroned in Gross by 3, undone in Stableford by his own R3 unravelling. New PBs at both PGA Catalunya courses — and somehow last.
Reigning Spoon holder shed the title the hard way: by being out-bottomed. An 11 at the Tour 15th, a course-worst 103 on the same card, and quintuples on four different cards — yet escaped last place.

Three-time Trophy winner reduced to colour: an 8 at R1 H5, a 10 at R2 H15 (followed by a birdie at 17 to stop the bleeding), and an 11-shot bleed in R4. The silver lining — 14 shots better than his last visit to the Stadium.

venue_notes: TEG's 2nd visit to Catalonia (after TEG 6 in 2012). Three rounds at PGA Catalunya (Stadium R1+R4, Tour R2 — both with five prior TEG visits, ripe for records) bookend a new-course excursion to El Prat - Azul on the Saturday. Use the Stadium's championship pedigree as backdrop to Jon Baker's pair of course records; flag El Prat as the only unfamiliar test and the round where the Trophy actually changed hands.
course_history_notes: Jon Baker's R1 90 at PGA Catalunya - Stadium beats the prior course best of 94 (set across 5 prior visits) AND is a 9-shot improvement on his own last visit
Jon Baker's R2 84 at PGA Catalunya - Tour beats the prior course best of 89 and is a 6-shot improvement on his own last visit
David Mullin set personal bests at BOTH PGA Catalunya courses (85 Tour in R2, 91 Stadium in R4) — and still finished last on Stableford
Gregg Williams was 14 shots better than his last visit to the Stadium course (113 in TEG 6 → 99 in R4)
John Patterson's R2 103 at the Tour course is the new course-worst, exceeding the prior worst of 101 across 5 prior visits
decisive_moments: Trophy: R4 H6 — Jon Baker draws level with Alex on the Stadium and never trails again across the closing 12 holes
Green Jacket: R3 H6 at El Prat - Azul — Jon Baker retakes outright Gross lead from Mullin and holds it through R4
Wooden Spoon: R3 H12 at El Prat - Azul — David Mullin's bogey drops him to last, and a R4 31 isn't enough to climb out

payoffs.seed: Jon Baker the eternal Jacket runner-up
payoffs.resolves_in: Round 4 + How the three were decided
payoffs.payoff: Wins the Jacket wire-to-wire by 3 — AND adds the Trophy. The drought ends with a double.
Held the outright Trophy lead from R3 H13; ceded it to brother Jon at R4 H6 and bled away to runner-up by 6.

payoffs.seed: David Mullin's eight Jackets and back-to-back run
payoffs.resolves_in: How the three were decided + the men in brief
payoffs.payoff: Dethroned in Gross by 3 — and collects the Wooden Spoon despite course PBs at both PGA Catalunya layouts.

payoffs.seed: John Patterson the reigning Spoon holder
payoffs.payoff: Wore last place from R1 H14 through R2, then Mullin's R3 unravelling lifted him off the bottom — Spoon shed, though with a course-worst 103 as a parting gift.
